subject: chemistry
title: Shape and polarity of water
given: oxygen is the central atom with two lone pairs
find: the molecular shape, bond angle and polarity
steps: Oxygen has two bonding pairs and two lone pairs: four electron groups.
Four groups are tetrahedral, but the two lone pairs leave a bent shape.
Lone-pair repulsion squeezes the H–O–H angle to about 104.5°.
The two O–H bond dipoles do not cancel in a bent shape, so the molecule is polar.
